title: codia triverticillata
intro: Codia triverticillata, also known as the Three-Leafed Codia or Wild Orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and fruits are used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Codia triverticillata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or used as a tincture to extract its medicinal compounds.
